name: Montenegro and Peña Hincada Loop
name_original: Viniegra de Abajo, Brieva de Cameros, Peña Hincada – circular
description: This difficult 28.3-mile mountain biking route offers challenging climbs, including Peña Hincada, and panoramic views in La Rioja.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Viniegra De Abajo offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de la Demanda and Urbión mountain ranges. The region features varied landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic river valleys carved by the Urbión or Frío river. This area provides a network of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels, characterized by its natural features and impressive mountain scenery. Best mountain bike trails around Viniegra De Abajo The most popular mountain bike trail is Ocejo and San Lorenzo Ascent Trail, a 22.4 miles (36.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valdezcaray and San Millán de la Cogolla Loop, a difficult 34.6 miles (55.8 km) path. This extensive loop traverses varied landscapes, including mountain passes and forested sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Ruta Duruelo Sur y Cabeza Alta – circular desde Duruelo de la Sierra, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through mountain and forest areas, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Viniegra De Abajo is defined by mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Demanda, river valleys, and diverse forest paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult ascents. The routes in Viniegra De Abajo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 8 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Viniegra De Abajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Viniegra De Abajo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Viniegra De Abajo offer diverse terrain, ranging from challenging mountain ascents and descents within the Sierra de la Demanda and Urbión mountain ranges to scenic river valleys carved by the Urbión or Frío river. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including forested sections and open mountain paths.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Viniegra De Abajo offers trails for various skill levels. There are 3 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058314327/ruta-duruelo-sur-y-cabeza-alta-circular-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta Duruelo Sur y Cabeza Alta – circular desde Duruelo de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993871771/ocejo-and-san-lorenzo-ascent-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ocejo and San Lorenzo Ascent Trail&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find trails that lead to stunning waterfalls, such as the Cueva Serena Waterfall and Cascada Paso Peñoncito. Many routes also offer impressive views of the Mansilla Reservoir, and you might pass by significant mountain features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5449542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo of the Laguna Negra de Soria&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Hincada Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748060/valdezcaray-and-san-millan-de-la-cogolla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdezcaray and San Millán de la Cogolla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 34.6-mile path traversing varied landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993871653/montenegro-and-pena-hincada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montenegro and Peña Hincada Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a challenging circular route.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: The best time to go mountain biking in Viniegra De Abajo is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be suitable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter can bring snow to higher elevations, making some trails challenging or inaccessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Viniegra De Abajo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from 8 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning mountain scenery, and the variety of trails that cater to different abilities, from leisurely river paths to strenuous mountain ascents.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While many trails in the region are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy routes available that could be suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These often follow river paths or less technical forest sections, providing a pleasant experience for all.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The area features several beautiful bodies of water. You can find routes that offer impressive views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4766914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mansilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable lakes in the vicinity include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5647099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Verde&lt;/a&gt;, which might be accessible via some trails or nearby.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748060/valdezcaray-and-san-millan-de-la-cogolla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdezcaray and San Millán de la Cogolla Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature an elevation gain of over 1800 meters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993871771/ocejo-and-san-lorenzo-ascent-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ocejo and San Lorenzo Ascent Trail&lt;/a&gt; also includes significant elevation gain, making it a demanding ride through mountain terrain.

Q: What are some longer mountain bike routes for extended rides?
A: For longer rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748060/valdezcaray-and-san-millan-de-la-cogolla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdezcaray and San Millán de la Cogolla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 34.6 miles (55.8 km) long and typically takes over 5 hours. Another extensive option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748484/villoslada-en-cameros-circular-por-senda-de-la-blanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villoslada en Cameros – circular por senda de La Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.8-mile (44.7 km) route.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, the village of Viniegra de Abajo itself offers historical charm with palatial houses built in ashlar stone and cobbled streets. While specific trails may not directly pass through historical sites, the cultural backdrop of the village adds another dimension to your outdoor adventure.
tips: The Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción is a construction made of ashlar masonry and reddish stone, characteristic of the area.

In addition to the church of the Assumption, this town has a rural house and beautiful houses belonging to families who returned from Latin America with a lot of money and who they called "los indianos". Cool off in the Cuatro Caños fountain.

In Viniega de Arriba, you have to leave the highway and continue straight along the street that runs through the urban complex, see the stone mansions, the cobbled streets, the fountains and the atmosphere that recalls the splendor of the glorious era of transhumance.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. The route includes a challenging climb to Peña Hincada, which features steep sections with gradients clearly exceeding 15%. Riders should have very good fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You'll encounter good asphalt roads, particularly on the ascent to Peña Hincada, but also significant unpaved sections. The area is mountainous, with some parts becoming quite exposed and stark after Brieva de Cameros. Be prepared for challenging climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular scenery. You'll pass through diverse landscapes, including oak forests and the beautiful Iregua valley. From the summit of Peña Hincada, you can enjoy a magnificent natural viewpoint with a spectacular panorama over the Sierra. The route also traverses areas near the Urbión or Frío river.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several interesting locations. You'll go through the settlement of Viniegra de Abajo and near the Viniegra de Arriba Historic Village. The Church of Our Lady of the Assumption is another highlight. The climb to Peña Hincada itself is considered a 'mythical Rioja port' for cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the village of Viniegra de Abajo, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for this circular route. As the trail is named after it, it's a logical place to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. During winter, especially at higher elevations like Peña Hincada, conditions can be exposed and challenging due to snow and ice. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the natural areas of La Rioja, including the Parque Natural del Alto Najerilla, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or in protected wildlife areas. Given the trail's difficulty and length, ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Parque Natural del Alto Najerilla. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private land.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will ride along parts of the Altos Valles Ibéricos, [CIMA LR03] Montenegro (Viniegras), [CIMA LR01] Peñahincada, and the GR 93, among others. This allows for a diverse riding experience through various established routes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate clothing layers for varied weather conditions, and sun protection. A helmet is essential, and a GPS device with the route loaded is highly recommended due to the varied terrain and potential for exposed sections.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: You will pass through villages like Viniegra de Abajo and Brieva de Cameros, where you may find small cafes or restaurants to refuel. However, options might be limited, especially outside of peak season. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient provisions and check opening hours in advance.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
